**Karşıyaka Basket** is a Turkish professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team located in [Karşıyaka](/wiki/Kar%C5%9F%C4%B1yaka "Karşıyaka"), [İzmir](/wiki/%C4%B0zmir "İzmir"), that competes in the [Turkish Basketball Super League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Super_League "Turkish Basketball Super League"). It is a section of the multi-sport club [Karşıyaka SK](/wiki/Kar%C5%9F%C4%B1yaka_SK "Karşıyaka SK"). The team also goes by the name of KSK, which stands for Karşıyaka Spor Kulübü (i.e. Karşıyaka Sport Club).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kar%C5%9F%C4%B1yaka_Basket&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Karşıyaka SK was founded in 1912 and opened its basketball section in 1966. Karşıyaka Basket is considered to be one of the most important basketball teams in all of Turkey. They first played in the [Turkish Basketball League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Super_League "Turkish Basketball Super League") in 1966. They relegated to lower divisions in 1968. The club also played in lower divisions from 1968 to 1974. The team is still participating in the Turkish Basketball League since 1974. Karşıyaka Basket was sponsored by Yaşar Holding's Food and Beverage Group; Pınar between 1998 and 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Their home arena is the [Karşıyaka Arena](/wiki/Kar%C5%9F%C4%B1yaka_Arena "Karşıyaka Arena") with a capacity of 6,500 seats, which was opened in 2005.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Big achievements with Sarıca

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kar%C5%9F%C4%B1yaka_Basket&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Big achievements with Sarıca")]

Karşıyaka set to name [Ufuk Sarıca](/wiki/Ufuk_Sar%C4%B1ca "Ufuk Sarıca") as head coach in the summer of 2012. Sarıca was dreamed to be in [Euroleague](/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball") with Karşıyaka and he said that "We have a dream and we want to be in Euroleague in 3 years." On February 9, 2014, the team won the [Turkish Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Cup_Basketball "Turkish Cup Basketball") with a victory against [Anadolu Efes](/wiki/Anadolu_Efes_S.K. "Anadolu Efes S.K.") under managing Ufuk Sarıca.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It was the first championship of club in Turkish Cup. On October 8, 2014, the team won the [President's Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_President%27s_Cup "Turkish Basketball President's Cup") by beating [Fenerbahçe Ülker](/wiki/Fenerbah%C3%A7e_Men%27s_Basketball "Fenerbahçe Men's Basketball"). In the [2014–15](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Turkish_Basketball_League "2014–15 Turkish Basketball League"), Karşıyaka won its second Turkish League after 1987 championship, despite finishing the regular season in the fourth place, by defeating Fenerbahçe Ülker in the playoffs semifinals by 3–1 and Anadolu Efes 4–1 in the finals.

On 29 July 2015, [EuroLeague](/wiki/EuroLeague "EuroLeague") announced the participating teams for the following season and included Karşıyaka with for the [2015–16 EuroLeague](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Euroleague "2015–16 Euroleague") season.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In its second appearance ever in the top tier of European basketball, the team won 3 games and lost 7.
